\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Header file containing global constants, structures, and function declarations for the solver. 

This file includes all necessary libraries, defines global constants, declares global variables, and provides function prototypes required for the solver. It also contains detailed documentation for the structures and functions used in the computational domain and boundary condition handling.\hypertarget{definitions_8h_Libraries}{}\doxysubsection{\texorpdfstring{Libraries}{Libraries}}\label{definitions_8h_Libraries}
Includes standard C++ libraries, VTK libraries, Boost, and JSONCPP for various functionalities.\hypertarget{definitions_8h_Global}{}\doxysubsection{\texorpdfstring{Constants}{Constants}}\label{definitions_8h_Global}
Defines mathematical constants, physical constants, and other macros used throughout the code.\hypertarget{definitions_8h_Structures}{}\doxysubsection{\texorpdfstring{Structures}{Structures}}\label{definitions_8h_Structures}

\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item {\ttfamily \doxylink{struct_boundary_info}{Boundary\+Info}}\+: Stores boundary information for different regions.
\item {\ttfamily \doxylink{struct_inlet_condition}{Inlet\+Condition}}\+: Represents inlet boundary conditions.
\item {\ttfamily \doxylink{struct_exit_condition}{Exit\+Condition}}\+: Represents exit boundary conditions.
\item {\ttfamily \doxylink{struct_initial_condition}{Initial\+Condition}}\+: Represents initial conditions for the simulation.
\item {\ttfamily \doxylink{struct_cell}{Cell}}\+: Represents a computational cell with properties, connectivity, and geometric attributes.
\end{DoxyItemize}\hypertarget{definitions_8h_Global}{}\doxysubsection{\texorpdfstring{Constants}{Constants}}\label{definitions_8h_Global}
Declares numerous global variables for storing simulation data, grid information, solver parameters, and boundary conditions.\hypertarget{definitions_8h_Functions}{}\doxysubsection{\texorpdfstring{Functions}{Functions}}\label{definitions_8h_Functions}

\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item \doxylink{class_grid}{Grid} and \doxylink{struct_cell}{Cell} Construction\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Functions for reading grid files, constructing cells, identifying neighbors, and computing centroids.
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item Solver Functions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Functions for solving inviscid and viscous flows, updating variables, and evaluating fluxes.
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item Boundary Conditions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Functions for applying various boundary conditions (e.\+g., wall, inlet, exit, symmetry).
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item Utility Functions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Functions for vector operations, sorting points, and evaluating gradients.
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item Output Functions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Functions for writing solution data, error files, and VTK files.
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item Test Case Functions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Functions for creating test case directories and handling specific test cases.
\end{DoxyItemize}
\end{DoxyItemize}\hypertarget{definitions_8h_Notes}{}\doxysubsection{\texorpdfstring{Notes}{Notes}}\label{definitions_8h_Notes}

\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item The file uses extensive use of {\ttfamily extern} to declare global variables and structures.
\item The file is designed to be included in multiple source files for modularity.
\item Ensure proper initialization of global variables before using them in the solver. 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition in file \mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_source}{definitions.\+h}}.

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Are\_Points\_Sorted\_AntiClockWise()}{Are\_Points\_Sorted\_AntiClockWise()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a501e947afd1bccc27ef4ee3d29234198_a501e947afd1bccc27ef4ee3d29234198} 
bool Are\+\_\+\+Points\+\_\+\+Sorted\+\_\+\+Anti\+Clock\+Wise (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ \mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_ae73d8ca7e27dfe59448bcfcbe537f514_ae73d8ca7e27dfe59448bcfcbe537f514}{V\+\_\+D}} $>$ \&}]{grouped\+Points}{, }\item[{const \mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_ae73d8ca7e27dfe59448bcfcbe537f514_ae73d8ca7e27dfe59448bcfcbe537f514}{V\+\_\+D}} \&}]{Centroid}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Sorts a set of points in anti-\/clockwise order around their centroid. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Points} & A flat vector of coordinates representing 3D points. The vector should have a size that is a multiple of 3, where each triplet (x, y, z) represents a point.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Assumptions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item The input vector {\ttfamily Points} is structured as a flat array of 3D points.
\item The function computes the centroid of the points and sorts them based on their angle relative to the centroid in the XY plane.
\item The Z-\/coordinate is ignored during sorting. 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_grid___computations_8cpp_source_l01250}{1250}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_grid___computations_8cpp_source}{Grid\+\_\+\+Computations.\+cpp}}.

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a51243b161eea5261235c049939418205_a51243b161eea5261235c049939418205}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!Calculate\_Face\_Average\_Flux@{Calculate\_Face\_Average\_Flux}}
\index{Calculate\_Face\_Average\_Flux@{Calculate\_Face\_Average\_Flux}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Calculate\_Face\_Average\_Flux()}{Calculate\_Face\_Average\_Flux()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a51243b161eea5261235c049939418205_a51243b161eea5261235c049939418205} 
void Calculate\+\_\+\+Face\+\_\+\+Average\+\_\+\+Flux (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const int \&}]{Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{N\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{Face\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{bool}]{Is\+\_\+\+Wall\+\_\+\+Face}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Calculates the average convective flux at a given face of a cell. 

This function computes the average flux at the interface between two cells using the left and right state variables. It also accounts for wall boundary conditions if the face is a wall face. The flux is calculated based on the velocity, pressure, and density of the cells, as well as the face geometry.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Cell\+\_\+\+No} & Index of the current cell (left cell). \\
\hline
{\em N\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No} & Index of the neighboring cell (right cell). \\
\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & Index of the face for which the flux is being calculated. \\
\hline
{\em Is\+\_\+\+Wall\+\_\+\+Face} & Boolean flag indicating whether the face is a wall face. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a37aa1bf90adb13316e2746f79d10d124_a37aa1bf90adb13316e2746f79d10d124}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!Calculate\_Flux\_For\_All\_Faces@{Calculate\_Flux\_For\_All\_Faces}}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Calculate\_Flux\_For\_All\_Faces()}{Calculate\_Flux\_For\_All\_Faces()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a37aa1bf90adb13316e2746f79d10d124_a37aa1bf90adb13316e2746f79d10d124} 
void Calculate\+\_\+\+Flux\+\_\+\+For\+\_\+\+All\+\_\+\+Faces (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{int \&}]{Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{void(\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{Dissipation\+\_\+\+Function~}{)(const int \&, int \&, const int \&)}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Evaluates flux on a given face. This function calculates the flux for a given face by considering the current cell number, its neighboring cell corresponding to the face, the face number, and whether the face is a boundary face. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The number of the current cell. \\
\hline
{\em Neighbour\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The number of the neighboring cell corresponding to the face. \\
\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & The face number for which the flux is being calculated. \\
\hline
{\em Is\+\_\+\+Boundary\+\_\+\+Face} & A boolean indicating whether the face is a boundary face.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Evaluates flux on a given face. This function calculates the flux for a given face by considering the current cell number, its neighboring cell corresponding to the face, the face number, and whether the face is a boundary face.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The number of the current cell. \\

\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & The face number for which the flux is being calculated. \\
\hline
{\em Is\+\_\+\+Boundary\+\_\+\+Face} & A boolean indicating whether the face is a boundary face. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Calculate\_Gradient\_On\_Face()}{Calculate\_Gradient\_On\_Face()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_aaba2caea764961926541016ae90e1092_aaba2caea764961926541016ae90e1092} 
void Calculate\+\_\+\+Gradient\+\_\+\+On\+\_\+\+Face (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const int \&}]{Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+Index}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{Grad\+\_\+\+Type}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{Face\+\_\+\+No}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Calculate the gradient on a face of a cell. 

This function calculates the gradient on a face of a cell using the provided cell index, gradient type, and face number.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+Index} & Reference to the index of the current cell for which the gradient is calculated. \\
\hline
{\em Grad\+\_\+\+Type} & Reference to the type of gradient to calculate (e.\+g., velocity, temperature, etc.). \\
\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & Reference to the face number (0 to 3) on which to calculate the gradient. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

\index{Condition\_For\_RICCA@{Condition\_For\_RICCA}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Condition\_For\_RICCA()}{Condition\_For\_RICCA()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_aa01e7e15e24ab2c77be345cececacfd0_aa01e7e15e24ab2c77be345cececacfd0} 
void Condition\+\_\+\+For\+\_\+\+RICCA (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{double \&}]{d\+\_\+U}{, }\item[{double \&}]{d\+\_\+F}{, }\item[{double \&}]{Vn\+\_\+L}{, }\item[{double \&}]{Vn\+\_\+R}{, }\item[{double \&}]{dP}{, }\item[{double \&}]{Rho\+\_\+I}{, }\item[{double \&}]{P\+\_\+I}{, }\item[{double \&}]{Alpha}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Computes the Alpha parameter for the RICCA condition based on input variables. 

This function calculates the Alpha parameter, which is used in the RICCA flux computation. It considers the velocity components, pressure differences, and other physical properties to determine the appropriate value of Alpha.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em d\+\_\+U} & Reference to the difference in conserved variables (e.\+g., density, momentum, energy). \\
\hline
{\em d\+\_\+F} & Reference to the difference in flux variables. \\
\hline
{\em Vn\+\_\+L} & Normal velocity on the left side of the interface. \\
\hline
{\em Vn\+\_\+R} & Normal velocity on the right side of the interface. \\
\hline
{\em dP} & Reference to the pressure difference across the interface. \\
\hline
{\em Rho\+\_\+I} & Density at the interface. \\
\hline
{\em P\+\_\+I} & Pressure at the interface. \\
\hline
{\em Alpha} & Reference to the Alpha parameter to be computed.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
If the pressure difference (dP) is below a small threshold (epsilon), it is set to zero. The function uses the maximum of the absolute values of the normal velocities (Vn\+\_\+L, Vn\+\_\+R) and incorporates a term proportional to the square root of the ratio of pressure to density at the interface, scaled by a constant (gamma).
\end{DoxyNote}
\begin{DoxyWarning}{Warning}
Ensure that the input parameters are properly initialized before calling this function. The function assumes that gamma is defined globally or accessible within the scope. 
\end{DoxyWarning}


Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_r_i_c_c_a_8cpp_source_l00027}{27}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_r_i_c_c_a_8cpp_source}{RICCA.\+cpp}}.

\DoxyCodeLine{00030\ \ \ \ \ \textcolor{comment}{//***************************************************************************************************************}}
\DoxyCodeLine{00031\ \ \ \ \ Alpha\ =\ 0.0;}
\DoxyCodeLine{00032\ \ \ \ \ \textcolor{keywordflow}{if}\ (dP\ <\ epsilon)}
\DoxyCodeLine{00033\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ dP\ =\ 0.0;}
\DoxyCodeLine{00034\ }
\DoxyCodeLine{00035\ \ \ \ \ Max\ =\ \mbox{\hyperlink{_source_8cpp_ad1286ce446bd24bcbdfecfe85808c943_ad1286ce446bd24bcbdfecfe85808c943}{max}}(fabs(Vn\_L),\ fabs(Vn\_R));}
\DoxyCodeLine{00036\ \ \ \ \ \textcolor{keywordflow}{if}\ ((fabs(\mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_a53a3ae3d84bb3ac66ffec5077cca7f49_a53a3ae3d84bb3ac66ffec5077cca7f49}{d\_F}})\ <\ epsilon\ and\ fabs(\mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_ad85ed9c1c30f75afe23a11a0d168e530_ad85ed9c1c30f75afe23a11a0d168e530}{d\_U}})\ <\ epsilon))}

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a4db2fe24c2a78f98ac91e9d7d271430f_a4db2fe24c2a78f98ac91e9d7d271430f}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!Evaluate\_Cell\_Net\_Flux\_2O@{Evaluate\_Cell\_Net\_Flux\_2O}}
\index{Evaluate\_Cell\_Net\_Flux\_2O@{Evaluate\_Cell\_Net\_Flux\_2O}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Evaluate\_Cell\_Net\_Flux\_2O()}{Evaluate\_Cell\_Net\_Flux\_2O()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a4db2fe24c2a78f98ac91e9d7d271430f_a4db2fe24c2a78f98ac91e9d7d271430f} 
void Evaluate\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+Net\+\_\+\+Flux\+\_\+2O (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Evaluates the net flux for each cell using a second-\/order flux calculation method. 

This function iterates over all physical cells and calculates the net flux for each cell based on the specified dissipation type. It initializes the net flux for each cell to zero, applies the appropriate flux calculation method for all faces of the cell, and evaluates the time step for the current cell.

\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
The function supports multiple dissipation types, each corresponding to a specific flux calculation method.
\end{DoxyNote}

\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item The dissipation type determines which flux calculation method is used\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item 1\+: LLF\+\_\+2O (Local Lax-\/\+Friedrichs, second-\/order)
\item 2\+: MOVERS\+\_\+2O (MOVERS, second-\/order)
\item 3\+: ROE\+\_\+2O (Roe\textquotesingle{}s method, second-\/order)
\item 4\+: RICCA\+\_\+2O (Ricca\textquotesingle{}s method, second-\/order)
\item 5\+: MOVERS\+\_\+\+NWSC\+\_\+2O (MOVERS with no wall shear correction, second-\/order)
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item The function also evaluates the time step for each cell after computing the flux.
\end{DoxyItemize}


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em None} & \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
void 
\end{DoxyReturn}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Identify\_Neighbours\_For\_Second\_Gradients()}{Identify\_Neighbours\_For\_Second\_Gradients()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a8d0266da95dde41c267e5f779a10c036_a8d0266da95dde41c267e5f779a10c036} 
void Identify\+\_\+\+Neighbours\+\_\+\+For\+\_\+\+Second\+\_\+\+Gradients (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{int \&}]{Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+Index}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Identifies the secondary neighbors of a given cell to evaluate second gradients. 

This function determines the secondary neighbors of the cell specified by {\ttfamily Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+Index}. Secondary neighbors are used for extended stencil calculations in gradient evaluation.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Current\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+Index} & Reference to the index of the current cell for which neighbors are identified. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a0a92a5cc2cf5822e9ee0db079e016f0c_a0a92a5cc2cf5822e9ee0db079e016f0c}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!MinMod@{MinMod}}
\index{MinMod@{MinMod}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{MinMod()}{MinMod()}\hspace{0.1cm}{\footnotesize\ttfamily [1/2]}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a0a92a5cc2cf5822e9ee0db079e016f0c_a0a92a5cc2cf5822e9ee0db079e016f0c} 
void Min\+Mod (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{double \&}]{a}{, }\item[{double \&}]{b}{, }\item[{double \&}]{phi}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Computes the Min\+Mod limiter for two input values. 

The Min\+Mod limiter is a slope limiter function used in numerical methods to ensure stability and prevent oscillations in solutions. It takes two input values, compares their magnitudes, and determines the limited value based on their signs and the smaller magnitude.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em a} & Reference to the first input value. \\
\hline
{\em b} & Reference to the second input value. \\
\hline
{\em phi} & Reference to the output value, which will store the result of the Min\+Mod limiter.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
This implementation assumes the existence of a {\ttfamily Sign} function that returns the sign of a number (-\/1 for negative, 1 for positive, and 0 for zero). It also uses the {\ttfamily fabs} function to compute the absolute value and the {\ttfamily min} function to find the smaller of two values. 
\end{DoxyNote}


Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_limiters_8cpp_source_l00055}{55}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_limiters_8cpp_source}{Limiters.\+cpp}}.


\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{00056\ \{}
\DoxyCodeLine{00057\ \ \ \ \ phi\ =\ 0.0;}
\DoxyCodeLine{00058\ \ \ \ \ \textcolor{keywordtype}{double}\ a1\ =\ fabs(a),\ b1\ =\ fabs(\mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_a3d57524b44f888354bc4d4f56e0e3f01_a3d57524b44f888354bc4d4f56e0e3f01}{b}});}
\DoxyCodeLine{00059\ \ \ \ \ phi\ =\ 0.5\ *\ (\mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_a956da109131dbcea0f4c96bb70aa0114_a956da109131dbcea0f4c96bb70aa0114}{Sign}}(a)\ +\ \mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_a956da109131dbcea0f4c96bb70aa0114_a956da109131dbcea0f4c96bb70aa0114}{Sign}}(\mbox{\hyperlink{definitions_8h_a3d57524b44f888354bc4d4f56e0e3f01_a3d57524b44f888354bc4d4f56e0e3f01}{b}}))\ *\ min(a1,\ b1);}
\DoxyCodeLine{00060\ \}}

\end{DoxyCode}
\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a99792d9196789915196e555da6bc5c3e_a99792d9196789915196e555da6bc5c3e}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!MinMod@{MinMod}}
\index{MinMod@{MinMod}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{MinMod()}{MinMod()}\hspace{0.1cm}{\footnotesize\ttfamily [2/2]}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a99792d9196789915196e555da6bc5c3e_a99792d9196789915196e555da6bc5c3e} 
void Min\+Mod (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{double \&}]{a}{, }\item[{double \&}]{b}{, }\item[{double \&}]{c}{, }\item[{double \&}]{phi}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Computes the Min\+Mod limiter for three input values and updates the result in the provided reference. 

The Min\+Mod function is used in numerical methods, particularly in finite volume and finite difference schemes, to compute a slope limiter. It ensures that the solution remains monotonic by selecting the minimum magnitude value among the inputs if they all have the same sign. If the inputs have mixed signs, the limiter is set to zero.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em a} & Reference to the first input value. \\
\hline
{\em b} & Reference to the second input value. \\
\hline
{\em c} & Reference to the third input value. \\
\hline
{\em phi} & Reference to the output value, which will be updated based on the Min\+Mod calculation.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
The function uses the following logic\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item If all inputs have the same sign and are non-\/zero, the minimum magnitude value is selected.
\item If all inputs are negative, the maximum magnitude value is selected.
\item If the inputs have mixed signs, the output is set to zero. 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_limiters_8cpp_source_l00021}{21}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_limiters_8cpp_source}{Limiters.\+cpp}}.

\DoxyCodeLine{00024\ \ \ \ \ \textcolor{keywordflow}{if}\ (a1\ >\ 0.0\ \&\&\ b1\ >\ 0.0\ \&\&\ c1\ >\ 0.0)}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Read\_Grid()}{Read\_Grid()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_ac092a2a6f3b32d1b4971c2f40907395f_ac092a2a6f3b32d1b4971c2f40907395f} 
void Read\+\_\+\+Grid (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const string \&}]{ipfile}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Reads the input grid file and constructs physical cells, evaluates face areas, normals, and cell volumes. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em ipfile} & The path to the input grid file.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Function for reading input grid file. This file has particular format written by me for reading the grid data. The grid file contains the information of the vertices of the cell followed by the cell and its neighbours information in .txt file. It takes a file containing data of grid and constructs Physical Cells, evaluates its face areas, normals and cell volumes. Creates the storage vectors for the above said variables. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_grid___computations_8cpp_source_l00139}{139}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_grid___computations_8cpp_source}{Grid\+\_\+\+Computations.\+cpp}}.

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a4d08f12e369b293abfbc5536d7ffba7c_a4d08f12e369b293abfbc5536d7ffba7c}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!RICCA@{RICCA}}
\index{RICCA@{RICCA}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{RICCA()}{RICCA()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a4d08f12e369b293abfbc5536d7ffba7c_a4d08f12e369b293abfbc5536d7ffba7c} 
void RICCA (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const int \&}]{Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{int \&}]{N\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{Face\+\_\+\+No}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Computes the dissipative flux for a given cell and face using the RICCA method. 

This function calculates the dissipative flux based on the left and right state variables of a cell interface. It uses the RICCA method to evaluate the flux differences and applies conditions for smooth regions.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The index of the current cell. \\
\hline
{\em N\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The index of the neighboring cell. \\
\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & The index of the face being processed. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}


Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_r_i_c_c_a_8cpp_source_l00057}{57}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_r_i_c_c_a_8cpp_source}{RICCA.\+cpp}}.

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_af68071625b590d44a51647290ccc1ee1_af68071625b590d44a51647290ccc1ee1}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!RICCA\_2O@{RICCA\_2O}}
\index{RICCA\_2O@{RICCA\_2O}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{RICCA\_2O()}{RICCA\_2O()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_af68071625b590d44a51647290ccc1ee1_af68071625b590d44a51647290ccc1ee1} 
void RICCA\+\_\+2O (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const int \&}]{Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{int \&}]{N\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{Face\+\_\+\+No}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Computes the dissipative flux for a given cell and face using the second-\/order RICCA method. 

This function calculates the dissipative flux based on the left and right state variables of a cell interface. It uses the second-\/order RICCA method to evaluate the flux differences and applies conditions for smooth regions.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The index of the current cell. \\
\hline
{\em N\+\_\+\+Cell\+\_\+\+No} & The index of the neighboring cell. \\
\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & The index of the face being processed. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}


Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{_r_i_c_c_a_8cpp_source_l00133}{133}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{_r_i_c_c_a_8cpp_source}{RICCA.\+cpp}}.

\Hypertarget{definitions_8h_a5bd379dc1f7a707fb647fe253ce630b6_a5bd379dc1f7a707fb647fe253ce630b6}\index{definitions.h@{definitions.h}!Second\_Order\_Limiter@{Second\_Order\_Limiter}}
\index{Second\_Order\_Limiter@{Second\_Order\_Limiter}!definitions.h@{definitions.h}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Second\_Order\_Limiter()}{Second\_Order\_Limiter()}\hspace{0.1cm}{\footnotesize\ttfamily [2/2]}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{definitions_8h_a5bd379dc1f7a707fb647fe253ce630b6_a5bd379dc1f7a707fb647fe253ce630b6} 
void Second\+\_\+\+Order\+\_\+\+Limiter (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const int \&}]{Cell\+\_\+\+Index}{, }\item[{const int \&}]{Face\+\_\+\+No}{, }\item[{vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{d\+\_\+U}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Computes the second-\/order limited slopes and reconstructs variables for a given cell and face. 

This function calculates the limited slopes using the Min\+Mod limiter and reconstructs the left and right states of the variables at the cell interface. It is used in finite volume methods to ensure stability and accuracy of the solution.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em Cell\+\_\+\+Index} & The index of the current cell. \\
\hline
{\em Face\+\_\+\+No} & The face number (0, 1, 2, or 3) corresponding to the interface direction. \\
\hline
{\em d\+\_\+U} & A vector to store the difference between reconstructed right and left states of variables.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
The function performs the following steps\+:
\begin{DoxyEnumerate}
\item Identifies neighboring cells and computes distances between cell centers.
\item Computes slopes using the specified slope method (Min\+Mod with two or three arguments).
\item Applies the limiter to the computed slopes.
\item Reconstructs the left and right states of variables at the interface.
\end{DoxyEnumerate}

The function uses the following helper lambdas\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item compute\+Slopes\+: Computes the slopes for the current variable.
\item apply\+Limiter\+: Applies the Min\+Mod limiter to the computed slopes.
\item reconstruct\+Variables\+: Reconstructs the left and right states of the variable at the interface.
\end{DoxyItemize}

The function supports four face directions\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Face\+\_\+\+No = 0\+: i-\/1/2, j interface
\item Face\+\_\+\+No = 1\+: i, j-\/1/2 interface
\item Face\+\_\+\+No = 2\+: i+1/2, j interface
\item Face\+\_\+\+No = 3\+: i, j+1/2 interface
\end{DoxyItemize}

\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
The function assumes that global variables such as {\ttfamily U\+\_\+\+Cells}, {\ttfamily Cells}, {\ttfamily Limiter\+\_\+\+Zeta}, and {\ttfamily Limiter\+\_\+\+Zeta1} are defined and accessible. It also assumes that the {\ttfamily Min\+Mod} function is implemented elsewhere. 
\end{DoxyNote}
